commands.repair_reason: The PR is active and contains useful migration work, but the remaining serviceUrl/cloud routing dispute and dependency freshness policy need maintainer judgment rather than an automation-only repair.; Needs attention: The diff still has concrete security and supply-chain concerns in Connector endpoint routing and dependency freshness policy.; Review finding: [P2] Preserve the stored serviceUrl for proactive sends —... (sha=cc8b9866a5a749677010b51166b7d8606acfabe2)
commands.review_summary:
This PR migrates the Microsoft Teams plugin from Bot Framework adapter/JWT/streaming plumbing to `@microsoft/teams.apps`, rewiring auth, invoke handling, streaming, proactive sends/edits/deletes, Docker defaults, tests, and package metadata.

Reproducibility: yes. The remaining blockers are source-reproducible: PR head drops stored `serviceUrl` when using SDK app-level send/API calls, and pnpm 10.33.2 treats a bare package-name freshness exclude as all versions.
commands.review_followup: The PR is active and contains useful migration work, but the remaining serviceUrl/cloud routing dispute and dependency freshness policy need maintainer judgment rather than an automation-only repair.

target.title: fix(msteams): rebase TeamsSDK patterns to simplify Teams Integration
